_comment: Salaried management roster. Annual figures are gross pay (excluding super). Deputy returns Cost=0 for all these employees because they're not on hourly rates in Deputy — this config lets the aggregator cost their shifts. Per Xero payroll (2026-07-15) a salaried employee is paid annual/52 EVERY week regardless of hours logged, so hours decide WHERE the cost lands (pro-rata across logged shifts), not how much it is. See scripts/wage_model.py.

297.name: Pujan Tamang
297._added: 2026-07-15 — found by Xero reconciliation. Salaried $75k, rosters in Deputy (Stow Kitchen, ~40h/wk), but was absent from this file so every shift costed $0 and his real cost fell into the corp-payroll residual.

_source_of_truth: Xero payroll, not Deputy. Deputy only knows who clocked on. Run scripts/check_salaried_roster.py to detect new salary-earners and salary changes — a salaried person missing from this file is booked at $0 and their real cost silently lands in the corp-payroll residual instead of on their venue.

_corp_payroll_only._comment: Owners. Salaried in Xero, never rostered in Deputy. Their pay belongs in corp payroll — which happens automatically: corpPayrollDaily() takes the residual of Xero group payroll MINUS Deputy group wages, so anyone absent from Deputy lands there by construction. Do NOT add them to `employees` — that would move owner salary onto venue wage lines. Listed here so the roster checker stops flagging them as missing.

_xero_exempt._comment: Deputy people Zak has CONFIRMED are correctly costed at Deputy's own rate because Xero does not pay them (2026-07-17: 'assume pedro at his deputy rates. same with long long. it's just an exception for pedro'). Listed by Deputy id so rebuild_wages stops flagging them. Anyone with hours who is NOT in Xero and NOT on this list gets shouted about — that is the point: the next person in this situation must not quietly blend into the fallback. Do NOT add someone here to make a reconciliation tie; add them only when Zak has said it's an exception.
